data.title: Why AEye Stock Is Trending Monday: New Leadership And Production Ramps
data.description:
• AEye stock is trending Monday. What should traders watch with LIDR?

What AEye’s Leadership Change Means for Growth

The company also approved an inducement grant of 125,000 restricted stock units, with one-third vesting in May 2027 and the rest vesting quarterly over the following two years. That structure signals AEye is paying to retain operations leadership through a multi-year production ramp.

Critical Price Levels for AEye Stock To Watch

AEye is sitting in the lower half of its 52-week range ($6.44 high, 50 cents low), which frames the current rebound as a recovery attempt rather than a return to prior peaks. The stock is trading 25.4% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 13.9% above its 100-day SMA, which points to improving short- and intermediate-term trend pressure even after a volatile year.

The moving average structure is still conflicted: the 20-day SMA remains below the 50-day SMA, and the death cross that formed in February (50-day SMA below the 200-day SMA) is a reminder that longer-term trend damage hasn't fully cleared. At the same time, price is only 2.4% below the 200-day SMA, so a clean reclaim of that long-term line is the technical "line in the sand" many investors watch for trend repair.

Momentum is leaning constructive on the moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum measure, with the MACD line above the signal line and a positive histogram, consistent with buyers having the near-term edge. In everyday terms, MACD being above its signal says upside momentum is currently stronger than downside pressure, even if it can still cool quickly.

  • Key Resistance: $2.50 — a level where rallies have recently tended to stall
  • Key Support: $2.00 — an area where buyers have shown up to defend pullbacks

What Is AEye And How Does It Operate?

AEye is a provider of high-performance, active lidar systems used in vehicle autonomy, driver-assistance systems and robotic vision. Its software-definable 4Sight Intelligent Sensing Platform combines solid-state active lidar, an optionally fused low-light HD camera and integrated deterministic AI to capture more useful information with less data.

That "scale into production" step is where operations and quality leadership matters, which helps explain why the market is reacting to an operations-focused executive hire. The company generates most of its revenue from the U.S., followed by Europe and Asia-Pacific, so manufacturing execution and supply chain consistency can directly affect how quickly it can convert programs into repeatable shipments.

AEye Earnings Preview: What To Expect In May

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the May 13 (confirmed) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 11 cents (Up from Loss of 33 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: 28 cents million (Up from 6 cents million YoY)

LIDR Stock Price Movement in Premarket Trading

LIDR Stock Price Activity: AEye shares were trading at $2.19 during premarket trading on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Courtesy of Aeye

data.title: Fulcrum Therapeutics Q1 2026 Earnings Call: Complete Transcript
data.description:
Fulcrum Therapeutics (NASDAQ:FULC) held its first-quarter earnings conference call on Monday. Below is the complete transcript from the call.

This content is powered by Benzinga APIs. For comprehensive financial data and transcripts, visit https://www.benzinga.com/apis/.

Access the full call at https://edge.media-server.com/mmc/p/pzpi4fi3/

Summary

Fulcrum Therapeutics reported positive clinical data from its Phase 1b Pioneer trial for Posterior Dare in sickle cell disease, showing an increase in fetal hemoglobin levels and a reduction in vaso-occlusive crises.

The company initiated an open-label long-term dosing trial for Posterior Dare and plans to provide updates on clinical trial design following an upcoming end-of-phase meeting with the FDA.

Financially, Fulcrum Therapeutics reported a net loss of $22.2 million for Q1 2026, with cash and marketable securities of $333.3 million, providing a runway into 2029.

Full Transcript

Alex Sapier (CEO and President)

Alan Musso (Chief Financial Officer)

Alex Sapier (CEO and President)

OPERATOR

Please stand by while we compile the Q and A roster. Our first question comes from the line of Joe Schwartz with Lee Rink Partners. Your line is now open.

Yes. Yeah, so this is. Yeah, and I can handle that one. So we've indicated that we expect to provide a fulsome reporting of the entire pioneer study at a medical conference later this year. We have not provided details on that as yet. As we indicated in the press release, the FSCDR symposium oral abstract will include previously disclosed clinical data.

Thanks so much for taking our questions.

Yeah, thanks. Anupam.

OPERATOR

Shannon, next question. Our next question comes from the line of Kristin Klasko with Kantor. Your line is now open.

Kristin Klasko

Alex Sapier (CEO and President)

No, I think you covered the key points really well, Alex. You know, given the severity of the disease and the unmet need, I think the recent progress in the sickle cell disease landscape is very encouraging for the field and we look forward to, to moving forward our program with posterior.

Kristin Klasko

Alex Sapier (CEO and President)

Yeah, Kristen, that's a great question. I think to answer that I will turn that one over to Ian, who has been deeply involved in the design as well as the execution of the open label long term dosing study that for which we've enrolled our first patient. Ian.

Thank you.

OPERATOR

I think the focus of the PK activators is on the increases in total hemoglobin resulting from a decrease in hemolysis and agree that the HBF mechanism, I think has emerged as a more central, more upstream mechanism to address more widely manifestations of sickle cell disease. And I think we're seeing that reflected in the interest in the clinic in sponsors that are bringing forward oral HBF inducers.

So that's certainly the first step on looking more globally and getting additional feedback on the program.

data.title: Bitcoin Braces For Fed Decision As Las Vegas Conference Kicks Off
data.description:
Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) is entering one of its most consequential weeks in months, with a convergence of macroeconomic policy, regulatory signaling, and industry catalysts that could shape near term price direction.

After briefly pushing toward the $80,000 level, Bitcoin has pulled back, with traders now shifting focus to a packed calendar that includes the Bitcoin 2026 Conference and an upcoming Federal Reserve rate decision.

The setup reflects a market that is no longer trading purely on crypto native narratives, but increasingly on macro liquidity, policy expectations, and institutional positioning.

Federal Reserve Decision Could Reset Risk Appetite

The biggest macro catalyst this week is the expected policy decision from the Federal Reserve.

Interest rates remain one of the most important drivers of crypto market behavior. Higher rates tend to tighten liquidity and pressure risk assets, while dovish signals often trigger rallies across equities and digital assets.

For Bitcoin, the stakes are elevated. The asset has shown a strong correlation with broader risk sentiment over the past year, particularly as institutional participation has increased through ETFs and structured products.

A hawkish tone from the Fed could reinforce the recent rejection near $80,000 and push Bitcoin into a consolidation phase. On the other hand, any संकेत of easing or future rate cuts could provide the liquidity tailwind needed for a breakout.

This dynamic has turned macro events into primary catalysts for crypto price action rather than secondary influences.

Bitcoin 2026 Conference Brings Industry and Policy Together

At the same time, the Bitcoin 2026 Conference is underway in Las Vegas, drawing major industry leaders, institutional investors, and policymakers.

Events like this have historically acted as sentiment drivers, but this year's conference carries added weight due to increased regulatory engagement.

One notable development is the participation of a sitting U.S. securities regulator, highlighting how digital assets are moving closer to the center of financial policy discussions.

That shift matters for markets. Regulatory clarity has become a key variable for institutional capital allocation, and even subtle changes in tone can influence flows into Bitcoin and other large cap assets.

For traders, the conference represents a real time gauge of how policymakers and industry leaders are aligning on the future of crypto infrastructure, compliance, and market structure.

Institutional Flows Remain the Underlying Support

Despite short term volatility, institutional demand continues to underpin the market.

Bitcoin's recent rally toward $80,000 has been supported by sustained inflows into investment vehicles, particularly spot ETFs. These flows have introduced a more stable base of demand compared to previous retail driven cycles.

However, that same institutional presence also increases sensitivity to macro conditions.

Unlike retail investors, institutional allocators are highly responsive to interest rates, inflation data, and broader portfolio risk considerations. This means Bitcoin is now more tightly linked to global liquidity cycles than at any point in its history.

The result is a market that can see strong inflows over multi day periods, but still experience sharp pullbacks when macro conditions shift.

A Market at an Inflection Point

Taken together, this week's events highlight a crypto market at a critical inflection point.

Bitcoin is no longer trading in isolation. Instead, it sits at the intersection of monetary policy, regulatory evolution, and institutional capital flows.

The combination of a major industry conference and a Federal Reserve decision creates a scenario where both narrative and liquidity drivers are in play simultaneously.

If macro conditions align with positive regulatory signals, Bitcoin could make another attempt at breaking above the $80,000 level, potentially opening the door to a new leg higher.

If not, the recent rejection may mark the beginning of a broader consolidation phase as the market digests both policy uncertainty and elevated positioning.

Why It Matters for Investors

For investors, the key takeaway is that crypto market timing increasingly depends on macro awareness.

Tracking Federal Reserve policy, understanding liquidity conditions, and monitoring institutional flows are now essential to navigating Bitcoin's price action.

This week offers a clear example of that shift.

The outcome of the Fed decision and the signals emerging from the Bitcoin 2026 Conference will likely set the tone not just for Bitcoin, but for the broader digital asset market in the weeks ahead.

Benzinga Disclaimer: This article is from an unpaid external contributor. It does not represent Benzinga’s reporting and has not been edited for content or accuracy.

data.title: What's Going On With OIO Group Stock Monday?
data.description:
Last week, the company completed a business combination with De Tomaso Automobili Holdings Limited, marking a significant shift in control.

Completes De Tomaso Deal

The completion of the business combination with De Tomaso was announced on April 23, 2026. The company positioned Norman Choi, De Tomaso’s founder, as the controlling shareholder of OIO Group.

He will assume the roles of CEO and chairman following completion of customary post-closing corporate actions.

The business combination with De Tomaso is a pivotal moment for OIO, as it aims to leverage De Tomaso’s expertise in the luxury automotive market to drive growth.

This strategic shift may position OIO to explore new opportunities in high-margin industries, enhancing its long-term value creation potential.

Reverse Stock Split

With the deal closure, the company confirmed a 1-for-3 reverse split of its ordinary shares. It became effective at the start of trading on April 24, 2026.

The move is intended to align the capital structure with Nasdaq listing requirements following completion of the business combination.

OIO Stock Price Activity: OIO shares were up 1.71% at $2.980 during premarket trading on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: Alliance Resource Partners Shares Holds Near Flat After Mixed Q1 Results
data.description:
• Alliance stock is holding steady today. What should traders watch with ARLP?

Q1 Highlights

Alliance reported adjusted earnings per share of 31 cents, missing the consensus estimate of $34 cents. In addition, it reported revenue of $516.01 million, beating the consensus estimate of $514.94 million, but representing a 4.5% year-over-year decline.

The company attributed the decline to lower coal sales pricing, partially offset by record oil and gas royalty revenues and higher coal sales volumes.

"Most of our coal operations performed better than expected during the quarter, however meaningful weather-related shipment disruptions relating to Winter Storm Fern delayed sales volumes for the quarter," said CEO Joseph W. Craft III.

Alliance ended the quarter with total liquidity of $431.2 million, including $28.9 million in cash and cash equivalents. 

Alliance declared a quarterly cash distribution of $0.60 per unit, equivalent to $2.40 on an annualized basis. 

Alliance Shares Edge Lower

ARLP Price Action: At the time of publication, Alliance shares are trading 0.52% lower at $24.77, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

This illustration was generated using artificial intelligence via Midjourney.
